REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA, LUCINDA PHILLIPS, RUBY ZUNIGA AND MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA XLIV'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR HOUSING DISCRIMINATION BASED ON DISABILITY (GOVT CODE SECTION 12955 (A); HOUSING DISCRIMINATION BASED ON RETALIATION (GOVT CODE SECTION 12955 (K); FAILURE TO REASONABLY ACCOMMODATE (GOVT CODE SECTION 12927(C)(1); UCL VIOLATION TO THE EXTENT IT ARISES OUT THE ISSUES COVERED BY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT (THIS RULING IS MADE IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE RULING ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE). THESE CLAIMS ARE BARRED AS THEY WERE EITHER PLEAD OF COULD HAVE BEEN PLEAD IN THE PRIOR LITIGATION AND THE PARTIES ADMITTED AND AFFIRMED THAT THEY HAVE BEEN FULLY LITIGATED AND DETERMINED IN THAT ACTION. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS OVERRULED. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY STATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; COURT REPORTER: JUDITH C. Eunice F. Chang for Fongkok Zhou, Shao Kun Zhou and Jack Keng Seng Zhou, Plaintiffs (via Zoom).
Christina M. Forst for Mercy Housing California, et al., Defendants.

Ms. Chang contested the court's tentative decision. After hearing oral arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE OF COURT TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 52.1 is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their minimal burden for leave to amend on the eve of continued trial, 4 years and 8 months after this action was filed, and discovery having been closed.
The Court further notes that in this motion plaintiff acknowledges that prejudice to the opposing party includes "delay of trial, loss of critical evidence, the added costs of preparation, and the increased burden of discovery. (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App. 4th 471, 486-488.)" and claims that there will be no additional costs. At the same time Plaintiff filed a Motion to Continue trial asserting that continuance is needed to conduct further discovery and "retain expert witnesses in support of violation of Civil Code 52.1." Late-filed reply is stricken.

Order signed in open court. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, CSRJane@hotmail.com, 909-831-2644 (D501)

MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFFS FONGKOK ZHOU, SHAO ZHOU AND JACK ZHOU'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION is DENIED. On June 24, 2021 the Court heard a demurrer to the First Amended Complaint. On that demurrer Defendant asked for judicial notice of the Settlement Agreement in prior litigation. Plaintiff did not object to the request for judicial notice. Therefore, in rendering its decision the Court considered the four corners of the complaint (which included no reference to the settlement, it effect or enforceability) and judicially noticeable documents (no objection) and their legal effect. Based on the review of appropriate filings on the demurrer, the Court allowed the Plaintiff to amend the complaint to plead around (in good faith) the provisions of the settlement agreement. Instead of doing so, Plaintiff filed this motion for reconsideration asking the Court to look at the extrinsic evidence (including multiple declarations) that could not have been properly considered on a demurrer to determine that the settlement agreement does not preclude the causes of action asserted in the First Amended Complaint. Even if the Court were to find that there are new facts, law or circumstances warranting reconsideration, the Court would not be able to consider any of the evidentiary arguments (e.g. intent, etc.) on its new re-considered demurrer ruling. All of these assertions shall be made in good faith in an amended complaint, as already authorized and ordered by the Court.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
